content.text: Now fix the routing pipeline steps in architecture.md (Steps 1-7 in doc, but actual code is Steps 1-5):

input.old_string:
```
Microsoft Graph API (Outlook)
    │  (polled every 15 min by OutlookIngestCronJob)
    ▼
emailMessage record created in workspace DB
    │  (@OnDatabaseBatchEvent → EmailRouterListener)
    ▼
EmailRouterService.route(emailMessage)
    │
    ├─ Step 1: domain lookup          (sender domain → company.routingDomain)
    ├─ Step 2: thread scan            (prior messages in same thread already routed?)
    ├─ Step 3: subject history        (prior messages with same subject line?)
    ├─ Step 4: company name fuzzy     (subject/body contains known company name?)
    ├─ Step 5: department narrowing   (multiple companies → pick by department keyword)
    ├─ Step 6: SO/PO regex            (subject contains PO# or SO# → map to opportunity)
    └─ Step 7: AI fallback            (OpenRouter call with company list context)
    │
    ▼
emailMessage.company, .department, .opportunity set
activityLog entry written
```
input.new_string:
```
Microsoft Graph API (Outlook)
    │  (polled every 15 min by OutlookIngestCronJob)
    ▼
emailMessage record created in workspace DB
    │  (@OnDatabaseBatchEvent → EmailRouterListener)
    ▼
EmailRouterService.route(emailMessage)
    │
    ├─ Step 1: domain lookup          (sender domain → company.routingDomain / domainNamePrimaryLinkUrl)
    │                                  skip if ACTIVE/POTENTIAL not found; skip if internal domain
    ├─ Step 2: department narrowing   (people on email scoped to exactly one department?
    │                                  requires person.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && person.departmentId)
    ├─ Step 3: PO/SO regex            (subject/body contains PO# or SO# → map to opportunity)
    ├─ Step 4: fuzzy name match       (subject/body ~= opportunity.name, threshold ≥ 0.5)
    └─ Step 5: AI fallback            (OpenRouter call with active programs list; UUID response)
    │
    ▼
emailMessage.companyId, .departmentId, .programId, .routingStatus set
```

`routingStatus` values: `ROUTED` (program found), `COMPANY_DEPT` (company + department, no program),
`COMPANY_ONLY` (company matched, no department), `UNROUTED` (no match), `SKIPPED` (internal sender
or `OTHER`-status company).

**Department attribution depends on `person.scope`.** The Step 2 filter is
`p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && p.departmentId`. `ContactAutoScopeListener` sets this automatically on
`person.created` and `person.updated`. If `scope` is NULL (e.g. from a direct-SQL insert), the email
will never be attributed to a department even if `departmentId` is set on the person.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Architecture

System design and data flow for the Pop Creations fork of Twenty CRM.

---

## System overview

```
Internet
  │
  ├─ crm.designflow.app (HTTPS) ──► Nginx (Coolify) ──► Twenty Server container
  │                                                           │
  │                                                    PostgreSQL (twenty-postgres)
  │                                                    Redis      (twenty-redis)
  │
  └─ Microsoft Graph API (Outlook polling)
       │
       └─► Worker container (BullMQ jobs + crons)
```

Two containers run from the same Docker image (`ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest`):

| Container | Command | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` | `node dist/main` | GraphQL API, webhook receivers, SSE, auth |
| `worker-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` | `node dist/queue-worker/queue-worker` | BullMQ job processor, cron runner |

The server registers crons (schedules BullMQ jobs) at startup. The worker executes them.

---

## Tech stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Jotai (state), Linaria (CSS-in-JS), Vite |
| Backend | NestJS, TypeORM, GraphQL (GraphQL Yoga), code-first schema |
| Queue | BullMQ (Redis-backed) |
| Database | PostgreSQL — `core` schema (global) + `workspace_<id>` schema (per-workspace) |
| Cache / session | Redis |
| Auth | Microsoft Entra OIDC (workspace login SSO), Microsoft OAuth for connected accounts |
| Build | Nx monorepo, Yarn 4, SWC compiler |
| Deploy | Docker, GHCR, Coolify API |

---

## Database layout

PostgreSQL has two schema types:

**`core` schema** — global tables (users, workspaces, apps, upgrades):
- `core.workspace` — workspace registry; production has one row (`99c80ca1-…`)
- `core.user`, `core.userWorkspace`, `core.apiKey`, `core.appToken`
- `core.upgradeMigration` — tracks applied v2.x upgrade commands

**`workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz` schema** — per-workspace tables:
- Standard objects: `company`, `person`, `opportunity`, `activity`, `note`, `task`, …
- POP custom objects: `emailMessage`, `department`, `factory`, `ignoreRule`,
  `licensorApprovalThread`, `meetingNote`, `meetingNoteAttendee`, `aiModelConfig`
- Every workspace table has an `__typename` discriminator column and soft-delete via `deletedAt`

The workspace schema name is derived from the workspace ID — do not assume it from env; query
`core.workspace` to find it.

---

## Custom object registration (v2.8 pattern)

Standard objects (both upstream and POP) are code-defined, not created via the UI.

1. **Metadata constant** — `packages/twenty-shared/src/metadata/constants/standard-object.constant.ts`
   registers each object's name, singular, plural, and field UUIDs under `STANDARD_OBJECTS`.
   Every entry must have a `views: {}` key.

2. **Workspace entity** — `pop-creations/standard-objects/<name>.workspace-entity.ts` defines the
   TypeORM entity with `@WorkspaceObject` / `@WorkspaceField` decorators.

3. **Field metadata util** — `field-metadata/pop-creations/compute-<name>-standard-flat-field-metadata.util.ts`
   registers the field metadata so the GraphQL schema generator can emit the correct types.

4. **Schema sync** — running `run-instance-commands` (or the upgrade command) applies the metadata
   diff to the workspace Postgres schema.

Custom fields on upstream objects (company, person, opportunity) follow the same pattern but the
workspace entity and metadata util live in the upstream files (see AGENTS.md §4).

---

## Email ingestion and routing pipeline

```
Microsoft Graph API (Outlook)
    │  (polled every 15 min by OutlookIngestCronJob)
    ▼
emailMessage record created in workspace DB
    │  (@OnDatabaseBatchEvent → EmailRouterListener)
    ▼
EmailRouterService.route(emailMessage)
    │
    ├─ Step 1: domain lookup          (sender domain → company.routingDomain)
    ├─ Step 2: thread scan            (prior messages in same thread already routed?)
    ├─ Step 3: subject history        (prior messages with same subject line?)
    ├─ Step 4: company name fuzzy     (subject/body contains known company name?)
    ├─ Step 5: department narrowing   (multiple companies → pick by department keyword)
    ├─ Step 6: SO/PO regex            (subject contains PO# or SO# → map to opportunity)
    └─ Step 7: AI fallback            (OpenRouter call with company list context)
    │
    ▼
emailMessage.company, .department, .opportunity set
activityLog entry written
```

Only companies with `customerStatus` of `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER` or `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER` are routing
candidates. `UNASSIGNED` and other statuses are excluded by design.

Internal senders (`@popcre.com`) skip routing.

---

## Cron jobs

All crons are registered by `CronRegisterAllCommand` (hardcoded list — not auto-discovered).

| Job | Schedule | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `OutlookIngestCronJob` | every 15 min | Poll Outlook mailbox via Microsoft Graph |
| `EmailRerouterCronJob` | every 30 min | Re-attempt routing for unrouted messages |
| `ClickUpSyncCronJob` | every 10 min | Mirror opportunity stage to ClickUp task status |
| `EmailContactSyncCronJob` | every 60 min | Sync email sender contact info to Person records |
| `MessagingMessagesImportCronJob` | upstream | Gmail/OAuth message import |
| `CalendarEventListFetchCronJob` | upstream | Google Calendar sync |
| … (20+ upstream crons) | various | Upstream Twenty background tasks |

Adding a new cron requires edits to **4 files** — see AGENTS.md §5.

---

## Authentication

**User login (workspace SSO):** Microsoft Entra OIDC. The `ENTERPRISE_KEY` env var (any non-empty
value) enables the OIDC/SSO guard. Employees authenticate with their `@popcre.com` Microsoft
accounts; Twenty issues its own session token. The OIDC provider record lives in
`core.workspaceSSOIdentityProvider` — see `docs/configuration.md` for the tenant/client/redirect
details.

**Microsoft OAuth (connected accounts):** separate from SSO. `AZURE_TENANT_ID` / `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` /
`AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` grant service-account access to Outlook and Calendar for the ingestion cron.
Individual users can also connect personal accounts via Settings → Connected accounts for personal
email/calendar sync.

**API keys:** generated via `generate-api-key` CLI command; stored in `core.apiKey`.

---

## Frontend overlay

POP frontend code lives in `packages/twenty-front/src/modules/pop-creations/`. It adds:
- Right-click context menu on `ParticipantChip` (navigate to People record)
- Company filter scoped to customer companies when filtering People records

The upstream Twenty frontend (React/Jotai/Apollo) is otherwise stock v2.8.3.

---

## Deployment topology

```
push to origin/main
    │
    ▼
GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/build-and-push.yml)
    lint → test → build Docker image → push ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest → trigger Coolify API
    │
    ▼
Coolify reads docker-compose.yaml from origin/main (pull_policy: always)
    │
    ▼
Coolify recreates server + worker containers on VPS
```

`docker-compose.yaml` on `origin/main` references `ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest`. Every push
to `main` builds a new image tagged both `latest` and `sha-<commit>` for auditability.

-    ├─ Step 6: SO/PO regex            (subject contains PO# or SO# → map to opportunity)
-    └─ Step 7: AI fallback            (OpenRouter call with company list context)

+    └─ Step 5: AI fallback            (OpenRouter call with active programs list; UUID response)
+**Department attribution depends on `person.scope`.** The Step 2 filter is
+`person.created` and `person.updated`. If `scope` is NULL (e.g. from a direct-SQL insert), the email
+will never be attributed to a department even if `departmentId` is set on the person.
Only companies with `customerStatus` of `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER` or `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER` are routing
candidates. `UNASSIGNED` and other statuses are excluded by design.

# Architecture

System design and data flow for the Pop Creations fork of Twenty CRM.

---

## System overview

```
Internet
  │
  ├─ crm.designflow.app (HTTPS) ──► Nginx (Coolify) ──► Twenty Server container
  │                                                           │
  │                                                    PostgreSQL (twenty-postgres)
  │                                                    Redis      (twenty-redis)
  │
  └─ Microsoft Graph API (Outlook polling)
       │
       └─► Worker container (BullMQ jobs + crons)
```

Two containers run from the same Docker image (`ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest`):

| Container | Command | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` | `node dist/main` | GraphQL API, webhook receivers, SSE, auth |
| `worker-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` | `node dist/queue-worker/queue-worker` | BullMQ job processor, cron runner |

The server registers crons (schedules BullMQ jobs) at startup. The worker executes them.

---

## Tech stack

| Layer | Technology |
|---|---|
| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Jotai (state), Linaria (CSS-in-JS), Vite |
| Backend | NestJS, TypeORM, GraphQL (GraphQL Yoga), code-first schema |
| Queue | BullMQ (Redis-backed) |
| Database | PostgreSQL — `core` schema (global) + `workspace_<id>` schema (per-workspace) |
| Cache / session | Redis |
| Auth | Microsoft Entra OIDC (workspace login SSO), Microsoft OAuth for connected accounts |
| Build | Nx monorepo, Yarn 4, SWC compiler |
| Deploy | Docker, GHCR, Coolify API |

---

## Database layout

PostgreSQL has two schema types:

**`core` schema** — global tables (users, workspaces, apps, upgrades):
- `core.workspace` — workspace registry; production has one row (`99c80ca1-…`)
- `core.user`, `core.userWorkspace`, `core.apiKey`, `core.appToken`
- `core.upgradeMigration` — tracks applied v2.x upgrade commands

**`workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz` schema** — per-workspace tables:
- Standard objects: `company`, `person`, `opportunity`, `activity`, `note`, `task`, …
- POP custom objects: `emailMessage`, `department`, `factory`, `ignoreRule`,
  `licensorApprovalThread`, `meetingNote`, `meetingNoteAttendee`, `aiModelConfig`
- Every workspace table has an `__typename` discriminator column and soft-delete via `deletedAt`

The workspace schema name is derived from the workspace ID — do not assume it from env; query
`core.workspace` to find it.

---

## Custom object registration (v2.8 pattern)

Standard objects (both upstream and POP) are code-defined, not created via the UI.

1. **Metadata constant** — `packages/twenty-shared/src/metadata/constants/standard-object.constant.ts`
   registers each object's name, singular, plural, and field UUIDs under `STANDARD_OBJECTS`.
   Every entry must have a `views: {}` key.

2. **Workspace entity** — `pop-creations/standard-objects/<name>.workspace-entity.ts` defines the
   TypeORM entity with `@WorkspaceObject` / `@WorkspaceField` decorators.

3. **Field metadata util** — `field-metadata/pop-creations/compute-<name>-standard-flat-field-metadata.util.ts`
   registers the field metadata so the GraphQL schema generator can emit the correct types.

4. **Schema sync** — running `run-instance-commands` (or the upgrade command) applies the metadata
   diff to the workspace Postgres schema.

Custom fields on upstream objects (company, person, opportunity) follow the same pattern but the
workspace entity and metadata util live in the upstream files (see AGENTS.md §4).

---

## Email ingestion and routing pipeline

```
Microsoft Graph API (Outlook)
    │  (polled every 15 min by OutlookIngestCronJob)
    ▼
emailMessage record created in workspace DB
    │  (@OnDatabaseBatchEvent → EmailRouterListener)
    ▼
EmailRouterService.route(emailMessage)
    │
    ├─ Step 1: domain lookup          (sender domain → company.routingDomain / domainNamePrimaryLinkUrl)
    │                                  skip if ACTIVE/POTENTIAL not found; skip if internal domain
    ├─ Step 2: department narrowing   (people on email scoped to exactly one department?
    │                                  requires person.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && person.departmentId)
    ├─ Step 3: PO/SO regex            (subject/body contains PO# or SO# → map to opportunity)
    ├─ Step 4: fuzzy name match       (subject/body ~= opportunity.name, threshold ≥ 0.5)
    └─ Step 5: AI fallback            (OpenRouter call with active programs list; UUID response)
    │
    ▼
emailMessage.companyId, .departmentId, .programId, .routingStatus set
```

`routingStatus` values: `ROUTED` (program found), `COMPANY_DEPT` (company + department, no program),
`COMPANY_ONLY` (company matched, no department), `UNROUTED` (no match), `SKIPPED` (internal sender
or `OTHER`-status company).

**Department attribution depends on `person.scope`.** The Step 2 filter is
`p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && p.departmentId`. `ContactAutoScopeListener` sets this automatically on
`person.created` and `person.updated`. If `scope` is NULL (e.g. from a direct-SQL insert), the email
will never be attributed to a department even if `departmentId` is set on the person.

Only companies with `customerStatus` of `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER` or `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER` are routing
candidates. `UNASSIGNED` and other statuses are excluded by design.

Internal senders (`@popcre.com`) skip routing.

---

## Cron jobs

All crons are registered by `CronRegisterAllCommand` (hardcoded list — not auto-discovered).

| Job | Schedule | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `OutlookIngestCronJob` | every 15 min | Poll Outlook mailbox via Microsoft Graph |
| `EmailRerouterCronJob` | every 30 min | Re-attempt routing for unrouted messages |
| `ClickUpSyncCronJob` | every 10 min | Mirror opportunity stage to ClickUp task status |
| `EmailContactSyncCronJob` | every 60 min | Sync email sender contact info to Person records |
| `MessagingMessagesImportCronJob` | upstream | Gmail/OAuth message import |
| `CalendarEventListFetchCronJob` | upstream | Google Calendar sync |
| … (20+ upstream crons) | various | Upstream Twenty background tasks |

Adding a new cron requires edits to **4 files** — see AGENTS.md §5.

---

## Authentication

**User login (workspace SSO):** Microsoft Entra OIDC. The `ENTERPRISE_KEY` env var (any non-empty
value) enables the OIDC/SSO guard. Employees authenticate with their `@popcre.com` Microsoft
accounts; Twenty issues its own session token. The OIDC provider record lives in
`core.workspaceSSOIdentityProvider` — see `docs/configuration.md` for the tenant/client/redirect
details.

**Microsoft OAuth (connected accounts):** separate from SSO. `AZURE_TENANT_ID` / `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` /
`AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` grant service-account access to Outlook and Calendar for the ingestion cron.
Individual users can also connect personal accounts via Settings → Connected accounts for personal
email/calendar sync.

**API keys:** generated via `generate-api-key` CLI command; stored in `core.apiKey`.

---

## Frontend overlay

POP frontend code lives in `packages/twenty-front/src/modules/pop-creations/`. It adds:
- Right-click context menu on `ParticipantChip` (navigate to People record)
- Company filter scoped to customer companies when filtering People records

The upstream Twenty frontend (React/Jotai/Apollo) is otherwise stock v2.8.3.

---

## Deployment topology

```
push to origin/main
    │
    ▼
GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/build-and-push.yml)
    lint → test → build Docker image → push ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest → trigger Coolify API
    │
    ▼
Coolify reads docker-compose.yaml from origin/main (pull_policy: always)
    │
    ▼
Coolify recreates server + worker containers on VPS
```

`docker-compose.yaml` on `origin/main` references `ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest`. Every push
to `main` builds a new image tagged both `latest` and `sha-<commit>` for auditability.

lines: | `OutlookIngestCronJob` | every 15 min | Poll Outlook mailbox via Microsoft Graph |
-| `EmailRerouterCronJob` | every 30 min | Re-attempt routing for unrouted messages |
-| `ClickUpSyncCronJob` | every 10 min | Mirror opportunity stage to ClickUp task status |
-| `EmailContactSyncCronJob` | every 60 min | Sync email sender contact info to Person records |
+| `EmailRerouterCronJob` | every 6 hours | Re-attempt routing for unrouted/company-only messages |
+| `ClickUpSyncCronJob` | 7am daily | Mirror opportunity stage to ClickUp task status |
+| `EmailContactSyncCronJob` | 2am daily | Sync email sender contact info to Person records |
| … (20+ upstream crons) | various | Upstream Twenty background tasks |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Deployment

Build, push, deploy, rollback, and verification for the Pop Creations fork.

---

## Infrastructure summary

| Component | Value |
|---|---|
| Host VPS | `178.156.180.212` |
| Coolify app UUID | `rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
| GHCR image | `ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest` |
| Coolify API (on-host) | `http://localhost:8000/api/v1` |
| Production URL | `https://crm.designflow.app` |
| Workspace DB schema | `workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz` |

Coolify reads `docker-compose.yaml` from `origin/main` and deploys the GHCR image referenced in
it. Because the compose uses `pull_policy: always`, every Coolify deploy pulls the latest image.

---

## Normal deploy — push to `main`

**Push a commit to `main`. GitHub Actions handles everything else.**

```
push to main
  → .github/workflows/build-and-push.yml
      lint → test → build Docker image → push to GHCR → trigger Coolify API
  → Coolify pulls ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest
  → server + worker containers updated
```

Required GitHub Secrets (already configured):

| Secret | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `COOLIFY_BASE_URL` | Coolify API base URL |
| `COOLIFY_API_TOKEN` | Coolify personal access token |
| `COOLIFY_SERVER_UUID` | App UUID (`rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92`) |

The workflow also publishes an immutable `sha-<commit-sha>` tag alongside `latest` and `main`
for auditability and rollback.

**Do not build Docker images manually on the VPS** — see `AI_OPERATING_RULES.md`.

---

## Verify deploy

```bash
# Cron registration log — expect "27 successful, 0 failed, 1 skipped"
docker logs $(docker ps -qf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92) 2>&1 | \
  grep "Cron job registration completed"

# Confirm all 4 POP crons registered
docker logs $(docker ps -qf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92) 2>&1 | \
  grep -E "OutlookIngest|EmailRerouter|ClickUpSync|EmailContactSync"

# Check server and worker are up
docker ps --filter name=rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92

# Tail server logs
docker logs -f $(docker ps -qf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92) 2>&1 | tail -50
```

---

## Rollback

A pre-cutover backup image is tagged `rollback-v120-20260603` in GHCR.

```bash
# Re-tag the rollback image as latest
docker pull ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:rollback-v120-20260603
docker tag ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:rollback-v120-20260603 ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest
docker push ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest

# Redeploy
TOKEN=$(cat /tmp/coolify_token.txt)
curl -s -X POST "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/deploy?uuid=rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92&force=true" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

Do not re-tag images by running `docker tag` on the production VPS for images already in
the running containers — use GHCR as the source of truth, push to GHCR, then redeploy.

---

## Database migrations

**POP migrations** are hand-applied SQL. The protocol:

1. Write a new numbered SQL file in `packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/migrations/`
2. Make it idempotent (safe to run twice)
3. **Commit the file first**
4. Apply to production:
   ```bash
   docker exec -i twenty-postgres psql -U twenty -d twenty \
     < packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/migrations/NNN_name.sql
   ```

**Upgrade commands** (framework schema changes) run automatically on server startup if
`DISABLE_DB_MIGRATIONS` is not set. The worker sets this to `true` — migrations only run in
the server container.

**To run an upgrade manually:**
```bash
docker exec $(docker ps -qf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92) \
  node dist/main run-instance-commands
```

---

## Backup

Nightly pg_dump at 23:15 UTC via systemd:

```
Timer:   popcre-twenty-backup.timer
Service: popcre-twenty-backup.service
Script:  /worksp/twenty/fork/ops/backup/create-backup.sh
Output:  /worksp/twenty/fork/backups/twenty_nightly_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>.dump
```

14-day rolling retention. Dumps are uncompressed PostgreSQL custom format (`-Fc`).

Manual backup:
```bash
bash /worksp/twenty/fork/ops/backup/create-backup.sh
```

Check backup health:
```bash
systemctl status popcre-twenty-backup.timer
journalctl -u popcre-twenty-backup.service --since "2 days ago"
ls -lh /worksp/twenty/fork/backups/
```

Restore:
```bash
# Stop server to prevent writes during restore
docker stop $(docker ps -qf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92)

docker exec -i twenty-postgres pg_restore -U twenty -d twenty --clean \
  < /worksp/twenty/fork/backups/twenty_nightly_TIMESTAMP.dump

docker start $(docker ps -aqf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92)
```

---

## SSH access

SSH to the VPS is allowed for:
- Inspecting container state and logs
- Emergency debugging
- Running one-off maintenance commands

SSH is **not** the deploy path (use Coolify API). SSH-editing source files on the server is
**forbidden** — changes don't survive the next image deploy.

---

## CI pipeline

`.github/workflows/build-and-push.yml` — active, triggers on every push to `main`.

Stages: `lint` → `test` → `build + push to GHCR` → `trigger Coolify deploy`.

The pipeline uses a **frontend fast path**: if all changed files are under `packages/twenty-front/`
or `packages/twenty-front-component-renderer/`, only frontend lint/test/build runs. Any other
changed file triggers the full suite.

Packages excluded from the test step: `twenty-front`, `twenty-zapier`, `twenty-e2e-testing`
(Playwright E2E requires a live server and cannot run headlessly in CI).

+`twentyhq/twenty-infra` using a `GH_TOKEN` that only twentyhq has. This workflow will always
+fail on the fork with exit code 4. **Ignore it.** Our real CI is `build-and-push.yml`.
`.github/workflows/build-and-push.yml` — active, triggers on every push to `main`.

content.text: Also add the Coolify queue retry procedure (it was needed today and could be needed again):

input.old_string: ## Rollback
input.new_string:
## Coolify deploy stuck / failed jobs

If `docker ps` shows containers that are many hours old and a recent build-and-push succeeded,
Coolify's `ApplicationDeploymentJob` may have failed and landed in the failed-jobs queue.

**Check and retry:**
```bash
# See failed jobs
docker exec coolify php artisan queue:failed

# Retry by UUID
docker exec coolify php artisan queue:retry <uuid>

# If nothing in failed queue, queue a fresh deploy directly
docker exec coolify php artisan tinker --execute='
use App\Jobs\ApplicationDeploymentJob;
$app = \App\Models\Application::where("uuid", "rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92")->first();
$q = \App\Models\ApplicationDeploymentQueue::create([
    "application_id" => $app->id,
    "application_name" => $app->name,
    "deployment_uuid" => \Illuminate\Support\Str::uuid(),
    "pull_request_id" => 0,
    "force_rebuild" => false,
    "is_webhook" => false,
    "status" => "queued",
    "server_id" => $app->destination->server_id,
]);
ApplicationDeploymentJob::dispatch($q->id)->onQueue("high");
echo "Queued: " . $q->deployment_uuid . "\n";
'
```

Watch for new containers:
```bash
docker ps --filter "name=rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92" --format "{{.Names}} {{.CreatedAt}}"
```

---

## Rollback

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Twenty CRM — Pop Creations Fork

Production CRM for Pop Creations (`popcre.com`) running at **[crm.designflow.app](https://crm.designflow.app)**.

This is a fork of [Twenty CRM](https://twenty.com) v2.8.3 with an inbound-email-routing business
system layered on top. Emails arrive in a shared Outlook mailbox, the system classifies them to the
correct customer company/department/program automatically, and surfaces them in the CRM timeline.

---

## Quick orientation

| Document | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **[AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md)** | Primary developer and AI guide — read this first |
| **[docs/architecture.md](./docs/architecture.md)** | System design, components, data flow |
| **[docs/development.md](./docs/development.md)** | Local setup, run/test/lint |
| **[docs/configuration.md](./docs/configuration.md)** | Env vars, auth (Microsoft Entra SSO, Microsoft OAuth) |
| **[docs/deployment.md](./docs/deployment.md)** | Build, push, Coolify deploy, rollback |
| **[HANDOFF.md](./HANDOFF.md)** | Open work: force-push to main + CI pipeline |
| **[CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md)** | Claude Code-specific notes |

---

## What this adds to upstream Twenty

- **Outlook ingestion** — cron polls a shared mailbox via Microsoft Graph every 15 minutes
- **Email router** — multi-step pipeline (domain → thread → subject history → fuzzy match → AI fallback)
  routes each message to the correct company/department/opportunity
- **8 custom objects** — `department`, `emailMessage`, `factory`, `ignoreRule`,
  `licensorApprovalThread`, `meetingNote`, `meetingNoteAttendee`, `aiModelConfig`
- **Extended standard objects** — `company` (customerStatus, chainType, routing fields),
  `person` (contactType, scope), `opportunity` (~23 program fields: PO#, factory, season, stage, etc.)
- **ClickUp sync** — opportunity stage mirrored to ClickUp task status
- **Fireflies webhook** — meeting transcripts auto-attached to the relevant company
- **AI summaries** — opportunity summaries generated via OpenRouter

Custom code lives entirely in:
- `packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/`
- `packages/twenty-front/src/modules/pop-creations/`
- `packages/twenty-shared/src/metadata/constants/standard-object.constant.ts` (POP entries)

---

## Repository layout

```
packages/
├── twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/   ← backend business logic
├── twenty-front/src/modules/pop-creations/    ← frontend hooks/components
├── twenty-shared/                             ← shared types; POP object metadata here
├── twenty-docker/twenty/Dockerfile            ← single image for server + worker
└── ... (upstream Twenty packages — do not modify without justification)
docker-compose.yaml                            ← lives on origin/main; read by Coolify
ops/backup/create-backup.sh                    ← nightly pg_dump (lives in fork worktree)
docs/                                          ← extended documentation
AGENTS.md                                      ← primary guide
HANDOFF.md                                     ← pending work
```

---

## Upstream Twenty

This project is based on [Twenty CRM](https://github.com/twentyhq/twenty) (MIT license). The upstream
documentation, roadmap, and community are at [twenty.com](https://twenty.com). This fork is not
affiliated with or endorsed by Twenty.

Done. Summary of what changed and why:

**`AGENTS.md`**
- §2: Removed stale `v28-refork` local branch note — branch is just `main` now
- §10: Two new quirks — (1) `CD deploy main` always fails on the fork (upstream workflow, not ours), (2) `person.scope` drives department attribution in the router and must be maintained by the listener
- §13: Two new incidents — `docker-compose.yaml` lost in the re-fork causing silent deploy failures, and the `person.scope` never-set bug causing 0 department attributions
- §14: Two items marked done

**`docs/architecture.md`**
- Fixed the routing pipeline step order to match actual code (Step 2 is department narrowing, not Step 5; Steps 3-5 are PO/SO, fuzzy, AI)
- Corrected cron schedules (rerouter is 6h not 30m, ClickUp is 7am not 10m, ContactSync is 2am not 60m)
- Added the `person.scope` contract for department attribution

**`docs/deployment.md`**
- Explained why `CD deploy main` always fails (don't chase it)
- Added Coolify stuck-deploy recovery procedure including the `tinker` commands used today

**`README.md`**
- Fixed stale HANDOFF description

**`HANDOFF.md`**: still exists and is correct — connected account re-auth (Albert + Adam, Settings → Connected accounts) is genuinely open and requires manual user action.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The session addressed four sequential tasks:

**Task 1 (Routing bug):** User reported "the routing is broken. We have a bunch of people routed to departments, but 0 emails routed to departments." Root cause found: `ContactAutoScopeListener` was writing to `companyCustomerStatus` instead of `scope`, so `person.scope` was always NULL. The email router's Step 2 filters by `p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT'`, so department attribution never worked.

**Task 2 (CD deploy failure):** User reported "CD deploy main #149 failed." Investigation revealed this was actually the harmless upstream Twenty workflow; our real deploy (`build-and-push.yml`) succeeded but Coolify was failing because `docker-compose.yaml` was lost during the v2.8 re-fork.

**Task 3 (Documentation):** User requested comprehensive documentation update reflecting actual current state of codebase derived from code, config, scripts, workflows, and session changes.

**Task 4 (Department field filtering — IN PROGRESS):** User reported "the dependency / relationship between Company and Department is not being enforced. For example Dept. sub section of the Email section. The department field is showing multiple 'Floor Coverings' values, multiple 'Desktop' values because each of them belongs to a different Company. What's shown in Department should be dependent on what's selected in Company. They are inextricably linked."

---

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **Pop Creations fork of Twenty CRM v2.8.3** at crm.designflow.app
- **NestJS workspace event system**: events are emitted as `{ properties: { after: T, before?: T } }` — NOT bare `Partial<T>` as old import paths implied
- **`@OnEvent` vs `@OnDatabaseBatchEvent`**: v2.8 uses workspace event bus; old `@OnEvent` pattern silently no-ops
- **`person.scope` field**: drives department attribution in the email router. Values: `DEPARTMENT`, `COMPANY_WIDE`, `IGNORED`. Set by `ContactAutoScopeListener`
- **Email router Step 2**: filters `p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && p.departmentId` to attribute emails to departments
- **Coolify deployment pipeline**: push to main → `build-and-push.yml` → GHCR → Coolify API trigger → `ApplicationDeploymentJob` queue
- **`docker-compose.yaml`**: Coolify reads this from GitHub on every deploy — must exist on `origin/main`
- **`filterOverride` pattern**: used in `useRecordsForSelect` and `ObjectFilterDropdownRecordSelect` for company-scoped person filtering; being extended to department picker
- **`SingleRecordPicker` chain**: `RelationManyToOneFieldInput` → `SingleRecordPicker` → `SingleRecordPickerMenuItemsWithSearch` → `useSingleRecordPickerRecords` → `useSingleRecordPickerPerformSearch` → `useObjectRecordSearchRecords`
- **`recordStoreFamilyState`**: Jotai atom family for accessing current record data from within field input components
- **`getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride`**: utility for computing filter overrides for POP-specific relation pickers

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

**`packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/logic-functions/contact-auto-scope/listeners/contact-auto-scope.listener.ts`**
- The critical bug file. Was writing `companyCustomerStatus` instead of `scope`.
- Full rewrite: fixed field name, fixed imports (now from `twenty-shared/database-events`), fixed `properties.after` access, added `person.updated` handler.
- Final code:
```typescript
import {
  type ObjectRecordCreateEvent,
  type ObjectRecordUpdateEvent,
} from 'twenty-shared/database-events';

@OnEvent('person.created', { async: true })
async handlePersonCreated(payload: ObjectRecordCreateEvent<PersonWorkspaceEntity>): Promise<void> {
  const entity = properties.after as Partial<PersonWorkspaceEntity>;
  const scope = this.determineScope(entity);
  await this.updatePersonScope(workspaceId, recordId, scope);
}

@OnEvent('person.updated', { async: true })
async handlePersonUpdated(payload: ObjectRecordUpdateEvent<PersonWorkspaceEntity>): Promise<void> {
  const before = properties.before as Partial<PersonWorkspaceEntity> | undefined;
  const after = properties.after as Partial<PersonWorkspaceEntity>;
  if (before && before.departmentId === after.departmentId && before.companyId === after.companyId) return;
  const scope = this.determineScope(after);
  await this.updatePersonScope(workspaceId, recordId, scope);
}

// updatePersonScope now writes to scope, not companyCustomerStatus:
const updateData: DeepPartial<PersonWorkspaceEntity> = {
  scope: scope ?? 'IGNORED',
};
```

**`docker-compose.yaml`** (restored at root of repo)
- Was missing from main branch — Coolify requires it for deployment
- Contains server + worker service definitions, Traefik labels, env var list, healthcheck
- Key: `image: ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest`, network: `coolify`, Traefik router UUID `rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92`

**`AGENTS.md`**
- §2: removed stale `v28-refork` local branch note
- §10: added two new quirks — upstream CD-deploy-main always fails; `person.scope` drives dept attribution
- §13: two new incidents — docker-compose.yaml lost in re-fork; person.scope never-set bug
- §14: two items marked done (docker-compose restore, department routing fix)

**`docs/architecture.md`**
- Fixed routing pipeline: Step 2 is department narrowing (not Step 5). Corrected steps:
  - Step 1: domain lookup
  - Step 2: department narrowing (requires `p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && p.departmentId`)
  - Step 3: PO/SO regex
  - Step 4: fuzzy name match
  - Step 5: AI fallback
- Corrected cron schedules (rerouter: 6h not 30m; ClickUp: 7am not 10m; ContactSync: 2am not 60m)
- Added `person.scope` contract documentation

**`docs/deployment.md`**
- Added section explaining `cd-deploy-main.yaml` always fails (upstream workflow, not ours)
- Added Coolify stuck-deploy recovery procedure with tinker commands

**`packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/utils/getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride.ts`**
- Added `companyId?: string | null` param
- Added `COMPANY_SCOPED_DEPARTMENT_SOURCES = ['emailMessage', 'meetingNote', 'opportunity']`
- New case: when source is emailMessage/meetingNote/opportunity and target is 'department', return `{ companyId: { eq: companyId } }` if companyId is set, else undefined (show all)
- Final code:
```typescript
const COMPANY_SCOPED_DEPARTMENT_SOURCES = ['emailMessage', 'meetingNote', 'opportunity'];

export const getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride = ({
  sourceObjectNameSingular,
  targetObjectNameSingular,
  customerCompanyIds,
  companyId,
}: GetPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverrideArgs) => {
  if (
    COMPANY_SCOPED_DEPARTMENT_SOURCES.includes(sourceObjectNameSingular ?? '') &&
    targetObjectNameSingular === 'department'
  ) {
    if (!companyId) return undefined;
    return { companyId: { eq: companyId } } as ObjectRecordFilterInput;
  }
  // ... existing company filter logic
};
```

**`packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/hooks/useSingleRecordPickerPerformSearch.ts`**
- Added `filterOverride?: ObjectRecordFilterInput` param
- Merges with `notFilter`:
```typescript
const combinedFilter =
  notFilter && filterOverride
    ? { and: [notFilter, filterOverride] }
    : (notFilter ?? filterOverride);
// passed to useObjectRecordSearchRecords({ filter: combinedFilter, ... })
```

**`packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/hooks/useSingleRecordPickerRecords.ts`**
- Added `filterOverride?: ObjectRecordFilterInput` param, passes to `useSingleRecordPickerPerformSearch`

**`packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/components/SingleRecordPickerMenuItemsWithSearch.tsx`**
- Added `filterOverride?: ObjectRecordFilterInput` to props type and component signature
- Passes to `useSingleRecordPickerRecords`

**`packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/components/SingleRecordPicker.tsx`**
- Added `filterOverride` to destructured props and passes to `SingleRecordPickerMenuItemsWithSearch`

**`packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-field/ui/meta-types/input/components/RelationManyToOneFieldInput.tsx`**
- PARTIALLY MODIFIED — imports added but logic not yet implemented:
```typescript
import { getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride } from '@/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/utils/getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride';
import { recordStoreFamilyState } from '@/object-record/record-store/states/recordStoreFamilyState';
import { useAtom } from 'jotai';
```
- Still needs: read `companyId` from `recordStoreFamilyState.atomFamily(recordId)`, compute filter, pass `filterOverride` to `SingleRecordPicker`

---

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **`contact-auto-scope.listener.ts` wrote wrong field**: `companyCustomerStatus` instead of `scope`. Fixed by changing the field name in `updatePersonScope`.
- **Old import paths don't resolve**: `src/engine/integrations/event-emitter/interfaces/object-record-create.event.interface` doesn't exist. Fixed by switching to `twenty-shared/database-events`.
- **Event `properties` structure**: Old pattern accessed `payload.properties` as bare entity. Actual runtime structure wraps in `{ after: T }` for creates and `{ before, after, diff, updatedFields }` for updates. Fixed access to use `.after`.
- **Prettier formatting hook**: Hook rejected commit. Fixed with `npx prettier --write` on the changed file.
- **git push rejected (non-fast-forward)**: Remote had commits ahead. Fixed with `git stash + git pull --rebase + git stash pop`, but stash pop had conflict on `2.8_upgrade.md` (deleted on remote). Resolved with `git rm 2.8_upgrade.md && git stash drop`.
- **SQL enum cast error**: `SET scope = 'DEPARTMENT'` failed — scope is a typed enum. Fixed with explicit cast: `'DEPARTMENT'::workspace_93r34ew9zc9644a9y5f1yeylz.person_scope_enum`.
- **Coolify `ApplicationDeploymentJob` failing**: Two jobs at 00:15:58 and 00:16:16 failed because `docker-compose.yaml` didn't exist. After restoring and committing the file, manually retried via `php artisan queue:retry <uuid>`, then queued fresh via tinker.
- **Coolify tinker constructor error**: `ApplicationDeploymentJob::__construct()` requires `int`, not the model object. Fixed by passing `$q->id` instead of `$q`.

---

5. Problem Solving:

**Email routing never attributed departments:** Traced the full pipeline — router Step 2 filters by `p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT'`, DB query confirmed 0 people had that scope despite 53 having departmentId. Found the listener bug (wrong field name), fixed code, backfilled DB directly.

**Coolify deploys silently failing for ~1 day:** Containers were 10+ hours old despite successful build-and-push runs. Found `ApplicationDeploymentJob` in Coolify failed queue. Root cause: `docker-compose.yaml` missing from main branch (lost in re-fork). Restored from git history, committed, manually queued fresh deploy via tinker.

**Department picker shows all companies' departments (IN PROGRESS):** A `filterOverride` mechanism exists for the filter sidebar path but not for the inline field input path (`RelationManyToOneFieldInput`). Threading `filterOverride` through 5 upstream files (4 done); `RelationManyToOneFieldInput` needs the final wiring.

---

6. All User Messages:

- "the routing is broken. We have a bunch of people routed to departments, but 0 emails routed to departments"
- "CD deploy main #149 failed"
- "Documentation is part of the work, not a final cleanup step. Update Markdown documentation to reflect the actual current state of the codebase..." [full documentation specification with required sections for AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, etc.]
- "the dependency / relationship between Company and Department is not being enforced. For example Dept. sub section of the Email section. The department field is showing multiple 'Floor Coverings' values, multiple 'Desktop' values because each of them belongs to a different Company. What's shown in Department should be dependent on what's selected in Company. They are inextricably linked."

**Security/operational rules (verbatim, must remain in effect):**
- Secrets (APP_SECRET, DB URLs, tokens) must never be printed or appear in AI context — write to `chmod 600` files or pipe only
- Do not probe `core.personal_access_tokens` or any auth table to extract credentials
- Do not dump full container environment variables (`docker inspect` / `docker exec env`)
- Force-push to `main` requires explicit, separate user authorization each time
- `docker exec` into a running production container to execute commands requires explicit authorization
- Never skip hooks (--no-verify) or bypass signing unless user explicitly requests
- Do not push unless the user asked for it
- SSH into VPS is allowed for inspection and emergency debugging — NOT the normal deployment path; SSH-editing source files on the server is forbidden
- Do not build Docker images manually on the VPS

---

7. Pending Tasks:

- **Complete `RelationManyToOneFieldInput.tsx` changes**: The imports were added but the actual logic to read `companyId` from `recordStoreFamilyState` and pass `filterOverride` to `SingleRecordPicker` is not yet implemented.
- **Update tests** for `getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride` to cover the new `emailMessage/meetingNote/opportunity → department` cases.
- **Build, push, and deploy** the completed frontend change.
- **Verify in browser** that the department picker filters correctly when a company is selected.

---

8. Current Work:

Implementing company-scoped department filtering for the relation field picker. The work is in the middle of modifying `RelationManyToOneFieldInput.tsx`. The imports have been added:

```typescript
import { getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride } from '@/object-record/record-picker/single-record-picker/utils/getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride';
import { recordStoreFamilyState } from '@/object-record/record-store/states/recordStoreFamilyState';
import { useAtom } from 'jotai';
```

But the component body still needs:
1. `const [recordInStore] = useAtom(recordStoreFamilyState.atomFamily(recordId));` to read the current record
2. Extract `companyId` from `recordInStore`
3. Compute `filterOverride` using `getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride({ sourceObjectNameSingular: objectMetadataItem.nameSingular, targetObjectNameSingular: fieldDefinition.metadata.relationObjectMetadataNameSingular, companyId })`
4. Pass `filterOverride` to the `<SingleRecordPicker>` component

The full chain through 5 files (`getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride.ts`, `useSingleRecordPickerPerformSearch.ts`, `useSingleRecordPickerRecords.ts`, `SingleRecordPickerMenuItemsWithSearch.tsx`, `SingleRecordPicker.tsx`) is already complete — only `RelationManyToOneFieldInput.tsx` body logic remains.

---

9. Optional Next Step:

Complete the `RelationManyToOneFieldInput.tsx` body changes. The file already has the necessary imports added. The next step is to add inside the component:

```typescript
const [recordInStore] = useAtom(recordStoreFamilyState.atomFamily(recordId));
const companyId = (recordInStore as Record<string, unknown> | null)?.companyId as string | null | undefined;

const departmentFilterOverride = getPopCreationsRelationPickerFilterOverride({
  sourceObjectNameSingular: objectMetadataItem.nameSingular,
  targetObjectNameSingular: fieldDefinition.metadata.relationObjectMetadataNameSingular,
  companyId,
});
```

Then pass `filterOverride={departmentFilterOverride}` to the `<SingleRecordPicker>` component. After that: run prettier, lint-check, commit, push to trigger CI/CD.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md

Claude Code-specific notes only. **[AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) is the primary operating guide — read it
first** (project summary, custom-code boundary, data model, deployment, quirks, pending work).
[README.md](./README.md) is the quick orientation. Allowed/disallowed operational actions:
**[AI_OPERATING_RULES.md](./AI_OPERATING_RULES.md)** — read before any deploy or production action.

## Ignore files

`.claudeignore` (Claude Code) and `.cursorignore` (Cursor) exclude vendored/generated/build dirs from
AI context — see AGENTS.md §9. With any other AI tool, paste AGENTS.md as your first message.

## Local Memory

`/home/ai/.claude/projects/-worksp-twenty/memory/`

Only store facts there that are likely to matter across multiple sessions and are not already
captured in repo docs.

## Operational Assumptions

- Claude Code runs directly on the VPS (`178.156.180.212`) with access to the repo and production tooling
- Execute deploy, runtime verification, and production investigation tasks directly — do not hand back runbooks
- SSH into the VPS is allowed for inspection, log collection, and emergency debugging — it must not be
  used as a routine deployment path (use the Coolify API instead); SSH-editing source files on the server
  is forbidden

## Production Identifiers

Full set in AGENTS.md §6–§7. Operational quick-reference:

| Item | Value |
|---|---|
| Coolify app UUID | `rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
| Server / worker container filter | `server-…` / `worker-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92` |
| Postgres access | `docker exec -i twenty-postgres psql -U twenty -d twenty` |

## Commit Style

- explain why the change exists, not what it does
- do not use `--no-verify`
- do not push unless the user asked for it

## Security

- Secrets (APP_SECRET, DB URLs, tokens) must never be printed or appear in AI context — write to
  `chmod 600` files or pipe only
- Do not probe `core.personal_access_tokens` or any auth table to extract credentials
- Do not dump full container environment variables (`docker inspect` / `docker exec env`)
- Force-push to `main` requires explicit, separate user authorization each time
- `docker exec` into a running production container to execute commands requires explicit authorization

## Tooling Notes

- Prefer deterministic repo commands over ad hoc shell mutations
- When production state is involved, verify both server and worker containers
- Treat repo, image, Coolify config, and live runtime as four separate layers that may disagree
- The Postgres MCP server (`.mcp.json`) is read-only — use it for inspection; use CLI commands for writes

input.prompt:
In /worksp/twenty/fork/packages/twenty-front/src/modules/object-record/record-field, find how a field cell decides whether it's editable/clickable. I want to understand:

1. What component renders the field cell (the clickable wrapper that opens the editor)?
2. Is there an `isEditable` or `disabled` or similar prop/state that prevents the field from entering edit mode?
3. How does the cell component use `useRelationField` or access the current record to conditionally control editing?

Look at: RecordTableCellContainer, RecordFieldCell, or similar wrappers. I want the file paths and relevant code snippets. Search breadth: medium.

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what was built:

- **`usePopCreationsDepartmentReadOnly` hook** — reads `companyId` from the record store and returns a predicate function. If the record is an emailMessage/meetingNote/opportunity, the department field reports `isReadOnly = true` when no company is set.
- **`RecordFieldList`** — ORs that predicate into `isRecordFieldReadOnly` for each inline field. Since department is a MANY_TO_ONE pulled into the inline loop for emailMessage records, it gets the grey/non-clickable treatment automatically.

Once deployed: the Department field will be visually greyed out and unclickable until a Company is first selected. After a company is selected, the picker opens and only shows departments belonging to that company.

content.text:
1,091 frontend files and 436 server files use Lingui macros. Ripping out `t\`...\`` from all of them would be ~1,500 file edits and high breakage risk. The practical wins with much less risk:

1. Delete all non-English `.po` locale files (the bulk of the weight)
2. Remove the language selector from app settings
3. Hard-pin the app to English

Let me check total locale file count and size:

content.text:
Clear plan:
1. Delete all non-English generated locale files (5.2MB in front alone)
2. Delete all non-English `.po` files
3. Hard-wire `initialI18nActivate` to always use English — no URL/storage/navigator detection
4. Remove the `LocalePicker` from settings (replace with static "English" display)

The `t\`...\`` macros stay in place — with English active they're zero-overhead pass-throughs. No 1,500-file rewrite needed.

# Delete all non-English .po files across all packages
find /worksp/twenty/fork/packages -name "*.po" ! -name "en.po" -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/dist/*" -not -path "*/.nx/*" -delete && \
find /worksp/twenty/fork/packages -name "*.po" -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/dist/*" -not -path "*/.nx/*"

content.text:
Pushed. 166 files changed, 819,210 lines deleted.

**What was removed:**
- 95 non-English `.po` source files across all packages
- 60 non-English compiled locale bundles (5.2MB front + 3.2MB server)
- `LocalePicker` component and its Language section in Appearance settings
- `I18nService` slimmed from 31 locale imports to 1

**What stayed intact:** All `t\`...\`` Lingui macros — they compile to English strings at build time with no runtime cost.

---

One side note: that commit also picked up three `.dump` files in `backups/` (51MB each, GitHub flagged them). Those database dumps shouldn't be in the repo. Want me to add `backups/` and `*.dump` to `.gitignore` and remove them from tracking?

content: Cleaning up the codebase: removed all non-English translations and locale files, and deleted upstream Crowdin workflows that were violating the branch rules. Next: remove the database dump files from git tracking and add them to .gitignore.

content.content:
1	# AGENTS.md
2	
3	Primary operating guide for developers and AI coding sessions on this repo. Read this first.
4	Then read the relevant file in `docs/` for the area you are working in.
5	
6	> There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
7	>
8	> `.claudeignore` works for Claude Code. `.cursorignore` works for Cursor.
9	>
10	> When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions
11	> in the **"9. What to ignore"** section below.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## 1. Project summary
16	
17	This is a **production fork of [Twenty CRM](https://twenty.com) v2.8.3** operated by Pop Creations
18	(`popcre.com`) under the designflow brand. It is live at **`https://crm.designflow.app`**.
19	
20	The business purpose is an **inbound-email-routing CRM**. A cron job polls a shared Outlook mailbox
21	via Microsoft Graph every 15 minutes, creates an `emailMessage` record for each message, and the
22	**email router** classifies it to the correct customer **company / department / program (opportunity)**
23	using a multi-step pipeline: domain lookup → thread scan → subject history → company name fuzzy →
24	department narrowing → SO/PO regex → AI fallback. Around that core the fork adds custom objects
25	(factories, licensor-approval threads, meeting notes), AI opportunity summaries, a Fireflies
26	meeting-transcript webhook, and a ClickUp status sync.
27	
28	All project-specific code lives in `pop-creations/` subtrees, leaving upstream Twenty files as close
29	to stock as possible. The outcome that matters: emails land on the right customer record automatically.
30

33	## 2. Repository structure
34	
35	This is the upstream Twenty monorepo (Nx + Yarn workspaces) with a project-owned overlay.
36	
37	| Area | Path | Ownership |
38	|---|---|---|
39	| **Our backend code** | `packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/` | project-owned |
40	| **Our custom-object metadata** | `packages/twenty-shared/src/metadata/constants/standard-object.constant.ts` (POP entries) + `packages/twenty-server/src/engine/workspace-manager/twenty-standard-application/utils/field-metadata/pop-creations/` | project-owned (lives in framework files) |
41	| **Our frontend code** | `packages/twenty-front/src/modules/pop-creations/` | project-owned |
42	| **Our SQL migrations** | `packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/migrations/` | project-owned (hand-applied) |
43	| **Docs** | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/`, `HANDOFF.md` | project-owned |
44	| **Deployment** | `docker-compose.yaml` (on `origin/main`), `packages/twenty-docker/twenty/Dockerfile` | project-owned |
45	| **Ops scripts** | `ops/backup/create-backup.sh` (lives in `fork` worktree, not refork) | project-owned |
46	| Upstream framework code | everything else under `packages/twenty-*` | third-party (Twenty) |
47	| Generated code | `packages/twenty-front/src/generated*/`, `**/locales/generated/`, `*/metadata/generated/` | generated — do not hand-edit |
48	| Build artifacts | `dist/`, `.nx/cache/`, `node_modules/`, `.twenty/` | not in scope (see §9) |
49	
50	**Branch model:** active development pushes directly to `origin/main`. CI triggers on every push
51	and deploys automatically. Treat `origin/main` as the single source of truth.

54	
55	## 3. Prime Directive: custom-code boundary
56	
57	**Our custom code lives here:**
58	
59	- `packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/`
60	- `packages/twenty-front/src/modules/pop-creations/`
61	- `packages/twenty-shared/src/metadata/constants/standard-object.constant.ts` (POP entries only)
62	- `packages/twenty-server/src/engine/workspace-manager/twenty-standard-application/utils/field-metadata/pop-creations/`
63	- `docs/`, deployment files
64	
65	**Everything else is upstream Twenty and requires justification before touching.** When a framework
66	file must change, make it **additive**, keep the diff **small**, and record it in §4. Do not spread
67	project logic across unrelated framework files — that is the most expensive upgrade-debt a session
68	can incur.
69	
70	Trust **active entry points** over directory shape: Nest module registration (`pop-creations.module.ts`),
71	`STANDARD_OBJECTS` in `standard-object.constant.ts`, and `CronRegisterAllCommand` tell you what
72	actually runs. The repo may contain historical artifacts — do not infer behavior from a folder alone.
73

97	## 5. Task-to-file navigation
98	
99	| Task | Files to touch | Files NOT to touch |
100	|---|---|---|
101	| Change email-routing logic | `pop-creations/services/email-router.service.ts` | core messaging engine |
102	| Change Outlook ingestion | `pop-creations/crons/jobs/outlook-ingest.cron.job.ts` | native `message-import-manager` |
103	| Add a custom object | New `pop-creations/standard-objects/<obj>.workspace-entity.ts` + new `field-metadata/pop-creations/compute-<obj>-standard-flat-field-metadata.util.ts` + register in `STANDARD_OBJECTS` in `standard-object.constant.ts` | other standard objects' metadata |
104	| Add a field to company/person/opportunity | The upstream `*.workspace-entity.ts` + matching `compute-*-standard-flat-field-metadata.util.ts` (see §4) | unrelated standard objects |
105	| Schema / data change | A **new numbered SQL file** in `pop-creations/migrations/` (applied manually; see protocol below) | already-applied migration files |
106	| Add a cron job | New `crons/jobs/<name>.cron.job.ts` + `crons/commands/<name>.cron.command.ts` → register in ALL THREE: `pop-creations.module.ts`, `jobs.module.ts`, AND `cron-register-all.command.ts` + `database-command.module.ts` | other cron jobs' logic |
107	| Add/modify an automation listener | `pop-creations/listeners/` or `pop-creations/logic-functions/*/listeners/` — use `@OnDatabaseBatchEvent` (v2.8); register in `pop-creations.module.ts` | upstream listeners |
108	| Add a frontend hook | `pop-creations/hooks/` | core hooks |
109	| Change deploy/build | `docker-compose.yaml` (on `origin/main` — see §12), `packages/twenty-docker/twenty/Dockerfile` | production `.env` (lives in Coolify) |
110	| Add an env var | `docs/configuration.md` + the consuming code | production env directly (set it in Coolify) |
111	
112	**Migration protocol (strict):** schema/data changes go into a **new numbered SQL file** in
113	`pop-creations/migrations/`. **Commit the file first, then apply it** with:
114	```bash
115	docker exec -i twenty-postgres psql -U twenty -d twenty \
116	  < packages/twenty-server/src/modules/pop-creations/migrations/NNN_name.sql
117	```
118	Migrations are **hand-applied SQL, not TypeORM**. Write idempotently (every migration here is re-runnable).
119	
120	**Cron registration is THREE places in v2.8** (unlike v1.20 which only needed `jobs.module.ts`):
121	1. `pop-creations.module.ts` — declares the provider
122	2. `engine/core-modules/message-queue/jobs.module.ts` — imports `PopCreationsModule` so the DI container can resolve the command
123	3. `database/commands/cron-register-all.command.ts` — adds the command to the hardcoded execution list
124	4. `database/commands/database-command.module.ts` — declares the provider for the database CLI context

214	
215	## 10. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
216	
217	### Server and worker share one image
218	
219	Looks like: they should be separate images.
220	Actually: one image, diverging by command and env. The server runs the default entrypoint; the worker
221	runs `node dist/queue-worker/queue-worker`. Worker has `DISABLE_DB_MIGRATIONS=true` and
222	`DISABLE_CRON_JOBS_REGISTRATION=true` to avoid duplicate startup side effects.
223	Why: simpler single-image build; upstream supports it.
224	Do not change because: it would double the build and break the current single-image deploy.
225	
226	### Cron registration requires THREE separate file edits in v2.8
227	
228	Looks like: registering in `pop-creations.module.ts` or `jobs.module.ts` should be enough.
229	Actually: `CronRegisterAllCommand` is a hardcoded list, not auto-discovery. Adding a cron command
230	requires it to be a provider in the DI container (via `jobs.module.ts`) AND explicitly listed in
231	`cron-register-all.command.ts` AND declared in `database-command.module.ts`.
232	Why: that is how upstream v2.8 wired the cron registry — it was not auto-discovered in this version.
233	Do not change because: changing the list mechanically removes the POP crons from registration.
234	
235	### Standard object IDs in `standard-object.constant.ts`, not entity files
236	
237	Looks like: field UUIDs should be in the `workspace-entity.ts` files.
238	Actually: In v2.8, every standard object and its field UUIDs are registered in
239	`packages/twenty-shared/src/metadata/constants/standard-object.constant.ts` under `STANDARD_OBJECTS`.
240	Each entry requires a `views: {}` key even if empty. Without it the metadata pipeline crashes.
241	Why: v2.8 moved field IDs to a shared constant to support cross-package metadata sharing.
242	Do not change because: removing or renaming entries breaks the workspace schema sync.
243	
244	### `@OnDatabaseBatchEvent` — not `@OnEvent`
245	
246	Looks like: `@OnEvent('opportunity.created')` from `@nestjs/event-emitter` should work.
247	Actually: v2.8 uses a workspace-event bus that batches records. The listener must use
248	`@OnDatabaseBatchEvent('opportunity', DatabaseEventAction.CREATED)` and read
249	`payload.events[].recordId` + `payload.events[].properties.after`. The old pattern silently no-ops.
250	Why: v2.8 changed the internal event architecture for multi-tenant workspace event dispatch.
251	Do not change because: reverting to `@OnEvent` means listeners never fire.
252	
253	### Routing only considers `ACTIVE_CUSTOMER` / `POTENTIAL_CUSTOMER` companies
254	
255	Looks like: a bug that excludes valid companies.
256	Actually: deliberate — routing against all companies produces mis-routing.
257	Do not change because: `UNASSIGNED` and other statuses must be excluded from routing candidates.
258	
259	### `ENCRYPTION_KEY` is satisfied by `APP_SECRET`
260	
261	Looks like: v2.8 requires a new `ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var.
262	Actually: `resolve-encryption-keys-or-throw.util.ts` resolves as `ENCRYPTION_KEY ?? APP_SECRET`.
263	If `APP_SECRET` is set (it is), no new variable is needed.
264	Do not change because: if someone adds `ENCRYPTION_KEY` separately, it must match the value used to
265	encrypt existing data or all encrypted fields (credentials, OAuth tokens) become unreadable.
266	
267	### Raw SQL in some pop-creations code paths
268	
269	Looks like: an ORM anti-pattern.
270	Actually: an intentional escape hatch. The TypeORM/workspace-ORM path silently returns 0 results for
271	cross-workspace queries initiated from the worker context. Raw SQL is the only reliable path there.
272	Do not change because: switching back to ORM silently no-ops.
273	
274	### `CD deploy main` workflow always fails on the fork
275	
276	Looks like: CI is broken and deploys aren't landing.
277	Actually: `cd-deploy-main.yaml` is the upstream Twenty workflow that dispatches to
278	`twentyhq/twenty-infra` — a repo we don't own. It requires a `GH_TOKEN` secret that only
279	twentyhq has. Our deploy pipeline is `build-and-push.yml` (lint → test → build → GHCR → Coolify).
280	Why: inherited from upstream; no practical way to disable it without maintaining a diff on the file.
281	Do not change because: it's harmless. Check `build-and-push.yml` for actual deploy status.
282	
283	### `person.scope` drives email department attribution
284	
285	Looks like: unused or redundant given `person.departmentId` already exists.
286	Actually: the email router's Step 2 (department narrowing) filters people by
287	`p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT' && p.departmentId`. Without `scope = 'DEPARTMENT'` the filter returns
288	zero matches and all department attribution is skipped even when `departmentId` is set.
289	Why: scope is set automatically by `ContactAutoScopeListener` (`person.created` and `person.updated`
290	events). If someone assigns a `departmentId` through a path that bypasses the ORM event (e.g.
291	direct SQL), they must also set `scope = 'DEPARTMENT'` or the router won't see the department.
292	Do not change because: the router depends on this contract. If changing the scope field name or
293	removing the listener, the routing pipeline must be updated in lockstep.
294	
295	### `@/` in twenty-front tests maps to `src/modules/`, not `src/`
296	
297	Jest `moduleNameMapper` maps `@/` → `packages/twenty-front/src/modules/`. Import a file at
298	`src/modules/object-record/foo/bar.ts` as `@/object-record/foo/bar`. The wrong path surfaces as
299	a misleading "Vitest cannot be imported in a CommonJS module" error — fix the path, not the phantom
300	ESM problem.
301

325	## 12. Deployment
326	
327	**Normal path — push to `main`, CI does everything:**
328	
329	```
330	push to main
331	  → GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/build-and-push.yml)
332	      lint → test → build Docker image → push to GHCR → trigger Coolify API
333	  → Coolify pulls ghcr.io/u2giants/twenty:latest → updates server + worker
334	  → VPS runs the containers (runtime host only)
335	```
336	
337	GitHub Secrets already in place: `COOLIFY_BASE_URL`, `COOLIFY_API_TOKEN`, `COOLIFY_SERVER_UUID`.
338	
339	**Do not build Docker images manually on the VPS** — those artifacts are untraceable, bypass all
340	verification gates, and violate the CI/CD rules in `AI_OPERATING_RULES.md`.
341	
342	**Verify deploy (from server logs after Coolify finishes):**
343	```bash
344	docker logs $(docker ps -qf name=server-rd261bt0wy7ifjrkoe1tkl92) 2>&1 | \
345	  grep "Cron job registration completed"
346	# Expect: 27 successful, 0 failed, 1 skipped
347	```
348	
349	**Rollback:** redeploy a previous immutable image tag through Coolify — do not `docker tag` on the VPS.
350	The pre-cutover backup image is tagged `rollback-v120-20260603` in GHCR.
351	See [docs/deployment.md](./docs/deployment.md) for the full rollback procedure.
352	
353	**SSH:** allowed for inspection and emergency log collection only. Never the deploy path. Never
354	edit source files on the server — changes won't survive the next image deploy.
355	
356	**Backup:** nightly pg_dump at 23:15 UTC via systemd timer `popcre-twenty-backup.timer`.
357	Script: `/worksp/twenty/fork/ops/backup/create-backup.sh`
358	Output: `/worksp/twenty/fork/backups/twenty_nightly_<timestamp>.dump` (14-day rolling retention)
359

362	## 13. Critical incidents
363	
364	### 2026-06-03 — OOM crash from stale staging containers
365	
366	What happened: During the v1.20→v2.8 upgrade, staging containers (`twenty-staging-server`,
367	`twenty-staging-postgres`, `twenty-staging-redis`) were left running alongside production after
368	cutover. Combined memory pressure (prod + staging + several other services) caused an OOM kill.
369	Impact: server unreachable for ~10 minutes; auto-restarted cleanly on reboot.
370	Root cause: multiple full Twenty stacks running on a 16 GB VPS.
371	Recovery: removed stale containers; server recovered automatically.
372	Rule added: after any staging work, `docker rm -f` all staging containers immediately on cutover.
373	
374	### 2026-06-03 — Backup service broken since 2026-05-14
375	
376	What happened: `popcre-twenty-backup.service` had `ExecStart` pointing to
377	`/worksp/twenty/app/ops/backup/create-backup.sh` — a path from a previous directory layout
378	that no longer existed. Exit 127 on every scheduled run.
379	Recovery: wrote new script at `/worksp/twenty/fork/ops/backup/create-backup.sh`, updated the
380	systemd unit, re-enabled timer. Confirmed working with a manual test run.
381	Rule added: after any directory reorganization, check systemd unit paths.
382	
383	### 2026-06-03 — POP cron jobs not scheduling (v2.8 registration gap)
384	
385	What happened: All 4 POP cron jobs (`OutlookIngest`, `EmailRerouter`, `ClickUpSync`,
386	`EmailContactSync`) were absent from `CronRegisterAllCommand`'s list. The server started fine
387	but logged only 23 crons registered — the 4 POP jobs were never scheduled.
388	Root cause: `CronRegisterAllCommand` is a hardcoded list, not auto-discovery. Adding
389	`PopCreationsModule` to `jobs.module.ts` alone (commit `5ebe86cc1d`) was necessary but not
390	sufficient — the commands also needed to be added to `cron-register-all.command.ts` and
391	`database-command.module.ts` (commit `eef74bd935`).
392	Rule added: see §5 cron registration note; §4 inventories both required file sets.
393	
394	### 2026-06-02/03 — v1.20 → v2.8 upgrade (re-fork + re-apply)
395	
396	What happened: straight merge of upstream v2.8.3 into the v1.20 fork produced 1,437 conflicts (88%
397	from upstream file deletions). Used a "re-fork" strategy instead: pinned v2.8.3 SHA as the new base,
398	then ported POP code on top.
399	Impact: production upgraded from v1.20 to v2.8.3 with zero data loss.
400	Recovery: two-step `run-instance-commands --force --include-slow` then `upgrade` (v1.20 chicken-and-
401	egg: `upgrade` queries `core.upgradeMigration` before it exists; `--force` skips that check).
402	Pre-upgrade backup: `twenty_preupgrade_20260602_204355.dump`; pre-cutover backup:
403	`twenty_cutover_20260603_075513.dump`. Both at `/worksp/twenty/fork/backups/`.
404	
405	### 2026-06-04 — `docker-compose.yaml` lost in re-fork; all Coolify deploys failing
406	
407	What happened: `docker-compose.yaml` (the file Coolify reads to know what to deploy) was not
408	carried forward when `main` was re-forked from v1.20 to v2.8.3. Every Coolify deployment since
409	the cutover was silently failing with "Docker Compose file not found at `/docker-compose.yaml`".
410	The file existed in the v1.20 git history (`a6d401906b`) but not in the new base.
411	Impact: containers running the v1.20 image (from pre-cutover) — no code changes deployed for ~1 day.
412	Root cause: re-fork creates a new git history; files not explicitly committed on `main` are absent.
413	Recovery: restored `docker-compose.yaml` from git history (commit `68341cc9b4`); manually triggered
414	Coolify deployment via tinker (failed jobs retried after two failures, then queued fresh).
415	Rule added: after any re-fork or forced push, verify `docker-compose.yaml` exists on `main` before
416	assuming deploys are working. Check `docker ps` container ages vs most recent deploy time.
417	
418	### 2026-06-04 — Emails never routed to departments (`person.scope` never set)
419	
420	What happened: `ContactAutoScopeListener` was setting `companyCustomerStatus` instead of `scope`
421	on newly created/updated people. All people had `scope = NULL` instead of `DEPARTMENT` /
422	`COMPANY_WIDE` / `IGNORED`. The email router's Step 2 (department narrowing) filters by
423	`p.scope === 'DEPARTMENT'` — so it always returned empty, making `departmentId` null on every email.
424	Impact: 0 emails routed to departments despite 53 people having `departmentId` set.
425	Root cause: copy-paste bug in `updatePersonScope()` — wrong field name. Additionally, the listener
426	only handled `person.created`, not `person.updated`, so manual department assignments never triggered.
427	The event `properties` access was also wrong (used `payload.properties` directly instead of
428	`payload.properties.after`).
429	Recovery: fixed the listener (commit `f4f732c663`); direct SQL backfill set `scope` correctly for
430	all 8,603 people (`DEPARTMENT`: 53, `COMPANY_WIDE`: 8,301, `IGNORED`: 249).
431	Rule added: when writing ORM event listeners, always access `payload.properties.after` (not
432	`payload.properties` bare). When a routing behavior is consistently zero across thousands of emails,
433	check that the filter predicate conditions are actually reachable in the data.
434	
435	### Invalid-variant UUIDs caused blank-page crash (pre-2026)
436	
437	What happened: hand-crafted UUIDs whose 4th group did not start with `8/9/a/b` were inserted,
438	crashing the frontend to a blank page.
439	Recovery: migration `001_enforce_uuid_variant.sql`. Rule: all hand-written UUIDs must be RFC-4122-valid.
440	
441	---
442	
443	## 14. Pending work
444	
445	| Status | Item | Next action |
446	|---|---|---|
447	| open | Connected account re-auth | Individual users (Albert, Adam) must go to Settings → Connected accounts → connect their personal Microsoft/Outlook accounts for personal email and calendar sync. Unrelated to workspace login SSO. |
448	| done | v1.20 → v2.8.3 upgrade | Completed 2026-06-03; crm.designflow.app live on v2.8.3 |
449	| done | Force-push `v28-refork` to `origin/main` | Done; CI auto-triggers on push to main |
450	| done | Wire CI/CD pipeline | `build-and-push.yml` live; push to main → lint → test → build → GHCR → Coolify |
451	| done | Phase E deferred frontend components | `useFieldListFieldMetadataItems.ts` ported; remaining 4 files were already at correct state in v2.8.3 base |
452	| done | Microsoft Entra SSO | Replaced Authentik OIDC with Microsoft Entra (`login.microsoftonline.com/1caeb1c0-…/v2.0`); redirect URI registered in Azure; employees sign in with `@popcre.com` Microsoft accounts |
453	| done | POP cron registration | All 4 crons registered; confirmed in server logs (27 successful) |
454	| done | Backup service fix | Nightly backup confirmed working; 14-day rolling retention |
455	| done | Staging container cleanup | All staging/cutover containers removed |
456	| done | Restore `docker-compose.yaml` | Lost in re-fork; restored from git history (commit `68341cc9b4`); Coolify deploys now work |
457	| done | Fix department routing (person.scope) | `ContactAutoScopeListener` bug fixed (commit `f4f732c663`); 8,603 people backfilled; emails now routed to departments |
458	
459	---
460	
461	## Documentation map
462	
463	- `README.md` — entry point and quick orientation
464	- `AGENTS.md` (this file) — primary developer/AI operating guide
465	- `CLAUDE.md` — Claude Code-specific notes only; points here
466	- `docs/architecture.md` — system design, components, data flow
467	- `docs/development.md` — local setup, run/test/lint workflow
468	- `docs/configuration.md` — env vars, auth, feature flags
469	- `docs/deployment.md` — deploy process, rollback, verification
470	- `HANDOFF.md` — in-progress work handoff (delete when resolved)
471	
472	Put guidance in one place and link to it. If a code change affects docs, update them in the same commit.
473
